title: corydalis erdelii subsp. nariniana
intro: Corydalis erdelii subsp. nariniana is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae, specifically a type of corydalis that is native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Corydalis erdelii subsp. nariniana include its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, fibromyalgia, and muscle spasms. Corydalis erdelii subsp. nariniana is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or capsule, and is used in traditional herbal medicine in various parts of the world.
